data.title: Inca Minerals Raises Voting Power in Stunalara Metals to 73.5%
data.description:
Inca Minerals raised its voting power in its takeover target, Stunalara Metals, to 73.5%, equivalent to acceptances totaling nearly 33.5 million undiluted shares, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Further, Andrew Haythorpe, who holds roughly 18.7% of Stunalara shares on an undiluted basis and 18.3% on a fully diluted basis, has confirmed his intention to accept Inca Minerals' takeover offer, per the filing.

Haythorpe's acceptance is subject to there being no superior proposal and the independent expert concluding that the offer is either fair and reasonable, or not fair but reasonable, the filing stated.

Inca Minerals' offer is subject to the bidder acquiring a relevant interest in at least 90% of all Stunalara shares on issue on a fully diluted basis by April 4, unless extended.

data.title: Goodwill Entertainment's Subsidiary Completes Share Subscription
data.description:
Goodwill Entertainment's wholly owned subsidiary, Yakitori One, completed the subscription of 480,000 new redeemable non-convertible preference shares and 20,000 shares, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Following the completion of the subscription, the subsidiary's issued and paid up capital is now SG$800,000.

data.title: Flynn Gold Resumes Drilling at Tasmania Project; Shares Hit 52-Week Low
data.description:
Flynn Gold resumed drilling at its Golden Ridge project in Tasmania, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The 2,000-meter diamond drilling program will test in-fill and extensional targets, with drilling at the Trafalgar prospect aiming to upgrade infilled areas of the exploration target to an inferred resource, the filing said.

Shares fell 9% in afternoon trade Thursday, earlier hitting a 52-week low.

data.title: Sinoma Science & Technology's 2024 Profit Declines 60%
data.description:
Sinoma Science & Technology's attributable profit slid 60% to 891.9 million yuan in 2024 from 2.22 billion yuan, after adjustments, in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Earnings per share at the advanced materials manufacturer decreased 60% to 0.5315 yuan from 1.3249 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income declined 7.4% to 24 billion yuan from 25.9 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares slipped 1% during Thursday's morning trading.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Dow Climbs After FOMC Interest-Rate Decision

Stocks rose after the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, while lowering its forecasts for growth this year.

Fed Dims Economic Outlook, Citing Uncertainty Over Tariffs

Officials extend pause on interest rates while projecting higher inflation and unemployment.

U.K. Is Expected to Follow Fed With Rate Pause. Why the BOE Really Wants to Cut.

The British economy has been much weaker than that of the U.S., and inflation is still well above the BOE target.

How Trump's talk with Putin about Ukraine involves this top U.S. industry

Trump wants Europe to buy more of America's liquified natural gas. But tariffs get in the way.

Australian Job Market Strength Showing Cracks

Australia's economy shed jobs in February in what could be the first clear indication in some time that interest rates have remained too high for too long.

New Zealand's Economy Returns to Growth; Outlook Uncertain

New Zealand's economy exited an extended period of recession in the final months of last year supported by hefty cuts in official interest rates, but the path ahead is uncertain for the small commodity-exporting nation given recent downgrades to world growth and a widening global trade war.

Canadian Consumers, Retailers Change Tack to Counter Tariff Stresses

Canadian consumers are responding to U.S. tariffs and presidential musings of annexation with a "Buy Canadian" movement that retailers and producers are swiftly embracing.

The Fed's interest-rate pause has a silver lining for worried Americans - if they make this move

Consumers are increasingly concerned about their jobs and their access to credit as recession fears loom.

Bonds Are Holding Up, but the Playbook Is Changing

As stocks slid in recent weeks, bond prices have ticked up, offsetting some losses

EU Targets 15% Cut in Steel Imports Amid U.S. Tariffs, Global Oversupply

The European Commission will protect the bloc's steel industry and introduce long-term measures to preserve its competitiveness in an industry awash in overcapacity globally.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Building-Products Distributor QXO Clinches Deal for Beacon Roofing

The agreement comes after a hostile takeover attempt.

SoftBank to Buy Ampere Computing for $6.5 Billion

The Japanese investment holding company said that the purchase would help to enhance its capabilities in key artificial-intelligence areas and accelerate its growth initiatives.

Bondi's Campaign Against Domestic Terrorism Starts With Tesla Foes

The attorney general is declaring a Justice Department crackdown on what she views as politically motivated attacks against Tesla CEO Elon Musk.

Chevron CEO Lobbies for More Time to Wind Down Venezuela Operations

Chief Executive Mike Wirth has lobbied top administration officials including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

Crypto Exchange Kraken Nears $1.5 Billion Deal for Futures Trading Business

NinjaTrader, a futures trading platform, would expand Kraken's offerings.

Ford Looks to Innovate Faster With AI Agents and Nvidia GPUs

Bryan Goodman, Ford Motor's director of artificial intelligence, said he's pushing forward a number of use cases for artificial intelligence at the company, as part of an effort to design and engineer new cars as quickly as its competitors.

Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner Resigns

The passenger railroad's chief executive is leaving the post after a little more than three years in the role.

Nissan to Cut 20% of Top Management Positions

Nissan said its top management positions will transition to a single-layer, non-officer framework, under which all corporate officers will assume the title of corporate executive.

Ben & Jerry's Says CEO Ousted by Unilever Over Brand's Social Activism

In a court filing, the ice cream maker said Unilever removed David Stever as CEO without approval from its independent board.

EU Pushes Ahead With Big Tech Antitrust Enforcement

The European Commission moved forward with enforcement of its digital antitrust rules on Apple and Alphabet's Google amid rising trade tensions with the U.S. over treatment of American tech giants.

data.title: Nvidia To Spend Hundreds Of Billions On US Chipmaking Over Next 4 Years, Says Chief- FT
data.description: • NVIDIA TO SPEND HUNDREDS OF BILLIONS ON US CHIPMAKING OVER NEXT 4 YEARS, SAYS CHIEF- FT

data.title: U.S. Tariffs Likely No Longer Dollar Bullish, Deutsche Bank Says — Market Talk
data.description: There are good reasons to argue that U.S. tariffs are no longer as dollar bullish as assumed at the start of the year, says George Saravelos, Global Head of FX Research at Deutsche Bank. With the historic German fiscal shift voted through it is very likely that the new German government front-loads fiscal easing to offset the impact of the U.S. tariffs, he says. Hence, the knock-on impact on growth is likely to be materially more muted. This conclusion, he says is valid across all of Europe, and the broader FX. Hence, the more proactive the fiscal reaction is from each impacted country, the more limited the growth and dovish central bank impact would be expected to be, he says. "By extension, we're holding on to the EUR/USD long through the [April 2] announcement," he adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Hovers at Record High
data.description:
Gold hovered around $3,050 per ounce on Thursday, a fresh record, supported by rising US rate cut expectations and strong safe-haven demand.

The Federal Reserve noted increased economic uncertainty but still expects to cut rates by 50 bps this year, matching its December projection and boosting non-yielding gold.

Meanwhile, tensions in the Middle East escalated as Israel launched “targeted ground activities” in Gaza, retaking part of a key area after an airstrike ended a two-month ceasefire with Hamas.

The US also kept striking Houthi targets in Yemen after Red Sea attacks, with Trump vowing to hold Iran accountable for future incidents.

At the same time, worries over global trade disputes persisted as new duties are set to take effect in April, following the US’s 25% tariff on steel and aluminum enacted in February.

data.title: Muyuan Foods Returns to Profit in 2024
data.description:
Muyuan Foods returned to an attributable profit of 17.9 billion yuan in 2024 from a loss of 4.26 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

The pork producer returned to earnings per share of 3.24 yuan from 0.79 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 24% to 137.9 billion yuan from 110.9 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares slipped 1% during Thursday's morning trading.

data.title: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem is speaking on Thursday
data.description:
1705 GMT / 1305 Eastern time:

  • Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem gives a speech at Calgary Economic Development
  • topic is: Tariff uncertainty and monetary policy
  • this will be followed by a Q&A

There will be a live broadcast (the kids call them webcasts now)

data.title: Sinopec Shanghai Petrochemical Swings to 2024 Profit, Revenue Down 6%
data.description:
Sinopec Shanghai Petrochemical swung to net profit attributable to shareholders of 316.5 million yuan, or 0.03 yuan per share, from a loss of 1.41 billion yuan, or 0.13 yuan per share, the previous year.

The petrochemical company's revenue was down 6.3% year over year to 87.1 billion yuan from 93.0 billion yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The company declared a dividend of 0.02 yuan per share for 2024.

data.title: The Trendlines Terminates Subsidiary Employee Involved in Funds Misappropriation
data.description:
The Trendlines terminated the employment of Anton Wibowo, chief executive officer of its subsidiary Trendlines Agrifood Innovation Centre, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

The termination follows the conclusion of an independent investigation into the extent of misappropriations between Jan 1, 2023, to Dec. 31, 2024.

Following the conclusion of these investigations, Anton was fired from his role while the group will now consider its legal options.

Meanwhile, Stephen Rhodes, the group's founder, has assumed the role of the subsidiary's CEO on an interim basis.

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC in Talks to Sell German Fund Administration Business
data.description:
HSBC Holdings is in advanced talks to offload its German fund administration business to Blackfin Capital Partners as it seeks to focus on its core Asian operations, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Blackfin is reportedly working on the final terms of the deal to acquire HSBC's Inka unit, which manages assets worth 400 billion euros. A deal may be reached in the coming weeks, the publication said, citing the people said.

BNP Paribas is also in negotiations for HSBC's German custodian business, Bloomberg said, citing sources. More interested parties may also negotiate for the platforms, which are expected to fetch several hundred million euros, Bloomberg said.

"We are reviewing our strategic options with regard to our securities services business in Germany," HSBC told Bloomberg. "No decision has yet been made, so we will not comment further on this."

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Hong Kong Keeps Base Rate at 4.75%.After Fed Decision
data.description:
The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) kept its base rate unchanged at 4.75% on January 30, hours after the US Federal Reserve maintained its borrowing costs, as widely expected, following three rate reductions in 2024.

Monetary policy in the Asian finance hub moved in line with the US as the local currency is pegged to the greenback.

The HKMA last cut the city’s interest rates by 25bps in December.

The central bank said in a statement that interest rates in Hong Kong might remain at relatively high levels for some time, and the extent and pace of future US interest rate cuts are subject to considerable uncertainty.

It added that the public should assess and continue to manage the interest rate risk when making property purchase, mortgage, or other borrowing decisions.

For years, high rates have placed a burden on the economy.

In Q3 of 2024, Hong Kong's GDP grew by 1.8%, the least in five quarters, amid a further drop in private consumption and moderation in goods exports.

data.title: Nera Telecommunications Winds Up Subsidiary in Myanmar
data.description:
Nera Telecommunications completed the winding up of its Myanmar-incorporated wholly owned subsidiary, Nera Telecommunications Myanmar.

The winding up is not expected to impact the company's earnings per share for the year ending Dec. 31, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: Palm gains on Chicago soyoil's rise, stronger crude
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures opened higher for the second straight session on Thursday, supported by rival soyoil's advances in the Chicago market and strength in crude oil.

The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 41 ringgit, or 0.93%, to 4,430 ringgit ($1,001.13) a metric ton in early trade.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) fell 0.54%, while its palm oil contract gained 0.44%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) advanced 0.21%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* Indonesia will raise its palm oil export levy to 4.5%-10% of the crude palm oil reference price from 3%-7.5% to finance a mandated increase in the amount of oil used in biodiesel, a plantation fund official said on Tuesday.

* According to independent inspection company AmSpec Agri, exports of Malaysian palm oil products fell 10.1% on-month to 396,865 tons during March 1-15, while Intertek Testing Services said it fell 7.5% to 420,677 metric tons.

* Malaysia has maintained its April export tax for crude palm oil at 10% and raised its reference price, a circular on the Malaysian Palm Oil Board website said.

* Oil climbed in early trade on a decline in U.S. fuel inventories and the escalating tensions in the Middle East.

* Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, strengthened 0.16% against the U.S. dollar, making the commodity more expensive for buyers holding foreign currencies.

MARKET NEWS

* Asia shares were hobbled by weakness in Chinese markets on Thursday and struggled to build on Wall Street's rally, even as investor sentiment was lifted by the prospect that the Federal Reserve could still deliver two rate cuts this year.

DATA/EVENTS

0100 China Loan Prime Rate 1Y, Loan Prime Rate 5Y Mar

0700 UK Claimant Count Unem Chng Feb

0700 UK ILO Unemployment Rate Jan

0700 UK HMRC Payrolls Change Feb

1200 UK BOE Bank Rate Mar

1230 US Initial Jobless Clm 15 Mar, w/e

1230 US Philly Fed Business Indx Mar

1400 US Existing Home Sales Feb

1430 US EIA-Nat Gas Chg Bcf, Nat Gas-EIA Implied Flow 14 Mar, w/e

($1 = 4.4250 ringgit)

data.title: Offshore Yuan Edges Lower, PBOC Holds Rates
data.description:
The offshore yuan edged lower to around 7.23 per dollar as investors digested the latest monetary policy decision from the People’s Bank of China.

The PBOC kept its key lending rates unchanged for the fifth consecutive month in March 2025, aligning with market expectations.

The one-year loan prime rate, a benchmark for most corporate and household loans, stood at 3.1%, while the five-year loan prime rate, which guides property mortgages, remained unchanged at 3.6%.

Both rates remain at historic lows following cuts in October and July of 2024.

Still, the PBOC remarked that it will lower interest rates and adjust the bank’s reserve requirement ratio at the appropriate time.

Meanwhile, Chinese policymakers recently unveiled a "special action plan" to boost consumer spending, though it lacked details on implementation.

data.title: Foreign selling in Indian stocks continues unabated in first half of March
data.description:
Foreign selling of Indian stocks continued in the first half of March, with information technology and consumer goods accounting for a significant share, amid concerns over the health of the U.S. and Indian economies.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) net sold Indian stocks worth $3.5 billion during the period. IT stocks accounted for 69.34 billion rupees ($803 million) of the selling, while consumer stocks worth $591 million were offloaded, exchange data showed on Thursday.

FPIs have sold Indian shares worth $28 billion between October and March, which has triggered a 13% fall in Nifty 50 from record high levels hit on September 27, 2024.

The IT index fell 3.2% during the first half of the month, compared to the benchmark Nifty 50's 1.2% rise. The IT index confirmed a bear market on March 12, which is defined as a decline of 20% from the recent peak.

Worries of a potential slowdown in the U.S. economy and inflationary concerns amid President Donald Trump's erratic tariff campaign have dimmed the prospects of a near-term recovery in earnings in the sector, said two analysts.

India's IT sector derives a large chunk of revenue from clients in the United States.

Earlier in the month, Jefferies double-downgraded India's IT sector to "underweight" from "overweight," citing high valuations and U.S. economic risks, while Citi said it is too early to be constructive on IT as recent U.S. data suggested a tough near-term outlook.

Meanwhile, recent domestic policy measures such as the government's cut to income tax and the Reserve Bank of India's interest rate cut and actions to boost liquidity failed to ignite investors' interest. The FMCG index rose 2.3% in the first half of March, after dropping 23% in the previous five months.

"Benefits of subsidies to low-income households and the tax-cuts for mid-income households are well understood, the sector lacks incremental triggers and is expensive on relative valuations compared to other sectors," said analysts led by Amish Shah of BofA India.

Among sectors, financials, oil and gas stocks have seen the highest foreign outflows since October, followed by consumer and auto stocks.

($1 = 86.3520 Indian rupees)

data.title: Czechia Designates Euro Manganese's Manganese Deposit a Strategic Deposit
data.description:
Euro Manganese said that the manganese deposit at its Chvaletice project was declared a strategic deposit by the Czech government, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The designation expedites and enhances the predictability of the permitting process, per the filing. It also streamlines coordination between various authorities and such deposits may qualify for special legislative procedures similar to those for key infrastructure projects.

The company secured the determination of the mining lease permit in January.

Euro Manganese's shares rose almost 3% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.description:
Here's a snapshot of Vietnamese dong exchange rates in the official and unofficial markets, indicative SJC gold prices in Hanoi and interbank offered rates at 0303 GMT.

For more interbank rate fixings released at 0400 GMT, click on (VNIBOR-1).

For Vietnam market overview click on: (VNDVIEW)

data.title: South Korean Won Holds Steady
data.description:
The South Korean won held steady around 1,460 per dollar as investors assessed the US Federal Reserve’s decision to keep rates unchanged while signaling two rate cuts this year.

Meanwhile, Fed Chair Powell downplayed the inflationary impact of Trump's tariffs, calling it likely short-lived or transitory.

Domestically, the won remained under pressure as political uncertainty deepened, following the Constitutional Court's decision to withhold a ruling date on President Yoon Suk Yeol's impeachment.

On the monetary policy front, the Bank of Korea’s latest meeting minutes signalled a potential economic slowdown, citing weak domestic spending and uncertainty over US trade policies.

However, a board member suggested the easing cycle may end sooner than expected to ensure financial stability, citing concerns over rising household debt and property prices.

data.title: Imeik Technology Development's 2024 Profit Rises 5%
data.description:
Imeik Technology Development's attributable profit rose 5.3% to 1.96 billion yuan in 2024 from 1.86 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

Earnings per share at the Chinese biomedical products manufacturer increased 5.7% to 6.50 yuan from 6.15 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 5.5% to 3.03 billion yuan from 2.87 billion yuan a year earlier.

data.title: YTO Express' Express Product Revenue Increases 36% in February
data.description:
YTO Express' express product revenue rose 36% year on year to 4.29 billion yuan in February, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The logistics company's business volume jumped 49% to 1.85 billion tickets.

Express product single ticket revenue fell 8.3% to 2.32 yuan.

Shares rose 2% during Thursday's morning trading.

data.title: Sanctioned crypto exchange Garantex shifts millions as it reboots platform
data.description:
Shuttered crypto exchange Garantex is reportedly back under a new name after laundering millions in ruble-backed stablecoins and sending them to a freshly created exchange, according to a Swiss blockchain analytics company.

Global Ledger claims the operators of the Russian exchange have shifted liquidity and customer deposits to Grinex, which they say is “Garantex’s full-fledged successor,” in a report released to X on March 19.

“We can confidently state that Grinex and Garantex are directly connected both onchain and offchain.”

“The movement of funds, including the systematic transfer of A7A5 liquidity, the use of one-time-use wallets, and the involvement of addresses previously associated with Garantex, provides clear onchain proof of their link,” the Global Ledger team said in the report.

After completing its investigation on March 13, Global Ledger says it had found onchain data showing Garantex laundered over $60 million worth of ruble-backed stablecoins called A7A5 and sent them to addresses associated with Grinex.

“In this case, the burning and subsequent minting process was used to launder funds from Garantex, allowing new coins to be minted from a system address with a clean history,” the team said.

A Garantex manager also reportedly told Global Ledger that customers have been visiting the exchange office in person and moving funds from Garantex to Grinex.

“Additionally, offchain indicators, such as transactional patterns, commentaries and exchange behaviors, further reinforce this connection,” it said.

The report also points to a description of Grinex on the Russian crypto tracking site CoinMarketRating, claiming that the owners of Garantex created it. The reports said this shows “Grinex is not an independent entity but rather a full-fledged successor to Garantex, continuing its financial operations despite the exchange’s official shutdown.”

By March 14, the volume of incoming transactions on Grinex was nearly $30 million, according to Global Ledger. CoinMarketRating shows that the trade volume for the month is now over $68 million, with spot trading topping $2 million.

The US Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control first hit Garantex with sanctions in April 2022 for allegedly money laundering violations.

On March 6, the US Department of Justice collaborated with authorities in Germany and Finland to freeze domains associated with Garantex, which they claim processed over $96 billion worth of criminal proceeds since launching in 2019.

Stablecoin operator Tether also froze $27 million in Tether (USDT), on March 6 which forced Garantex to halt all operations, including withdrawals.

Only a few days later, on March 12, officials with India’s Central Bureau of Investigation arrested Aleksej Bešciokov, who allegedly operated Garantex, on US charges that included conspiracy to commit money laundering.

data.title: Banks lift Australia shares after Fed's Powell comments calm tariff jitters
data.description:
Australian shares advanced 1% on Thursday, led by banks after the U.S. Federal Reserve hinted it would reduce borrowing costs by half a percentage point by the end of the year, calming investors rattled by the Trump administration's tariff rollouts.

Meanwhile, local data showed a surprise fall in jobs in February, indicating a loosening labour market, though the prospects of interest rate cuts this year by the country's central bank remained unchanged.

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 1.1% to 7,916.1, as of 0213 GMT. The benchmark closed 0.4% lower on Wednesday.

Annual Australian jobs growth in February pulled back sharply to just 1.9%, from 3.5% the previous month, and figures showed net employment fell 52,800 in February from January.

The Reserve Bank of Australia had cautioned further easing could not be guaranteed given the surprisingly strong labour market could risk stoking inflation.

The main inflationary effect of strong employment is typically through rising wages, but wage growth moderated to a two-year low in the last quarter.

In Sydney, banks gained 1.8%, snapping a two-day losing streak. All the "big four" lenders gained between 1.4% and 1.9%.

Gold stocks rose 2.1%, aided by bullion prices reaching a fresh high.

Miners Northern Star Resources and Evolution Mining gained 2.8%, each.

Information technology stocks tracked Wall Street gains, rising 1.6%. Sector majors WiseTech Global , Xero and Block were up between 1.1% and 1.8%.

The gains were, however, capped by losses in miners , after iron ore prices eased overnight.

TPG Telecom jumped 5.3% after the local competition watchdog backed its $3.3 billion deal with telecoms company Vocus.

Meanwhile, New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index traded 0.2% higher at 12,070.84.

Fonterra , the world's largest dairy exporter, added 2.3% after it reported strong first-half earnings and lifted its dividend.

data.title: Japanese Yen Strengthens on Dovish Fed Signals
data.description:
The Japanese yen appreciated past 148.5 per dollar on Thursday, rising for the second straight session as the dollar weakened after the US Federal Reserve reaffirmed its outlook for two interest rate cuts this year.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell also downplayed the inflationary impact of President Donald Trump’s tariffs, calling them as likely short-lived or transitory.

Meanwhile, the Bank of Japan held its policy rate steady at 0.5% on Wednesday, as expected.

Policymakers signaled a cautious stance, opting to assess the impact of global economic risks, particularly higher US tariffs, on Japan’s economy.

The BOJ also emphasized its close monitoring of foreign exchange markets and their influence on economic conditions and prices.

data.title: Beijing Kingsoft Office Software's 2024 Profit Jumps 25%; Shares Slip 3%
data.description:
Beijing Kingsoft Office Software attributable profit rose 25% to 1.65 billion yuan in 2024 from 1.32 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

Earnings per share at the office software company increased 25% to 3.56 yuan from 2.85 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 12% to 5.12 billion yuan from 4.56 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares fell 3% during Thursday's morning trading.

data.title: China National Medicines' 2024 Profit Down 7%, Revenue Up 2%
data.description:
China National Medicines posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 2.00 billion yuan, or 2.6509 yuan per share, down 6.8% from 2.15 billion yuan, or 2.8443 yuan per share, the previous year.

The revenue of the Sinopharm Group unit climbed 1.8% year over year to 50.6 billion yuan from 49.7 billion yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The company declared a cash dividend of 0.80 per share for 2024.

Shares of China National Medicines were down 2% in recent trade.
